Address.title: Address
Address.body:
import React, { useMemo } from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { DocsProvider } from "../../components/DocsProvider";

# Address Component

The `Address` component displays EVM-format addresses with a blockie, copy control, and optional block explorer link (Hedera-aware when `chain` is a Hedera network).

## Import

```tsx
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
```

## Props

| Prop                       | Type                                                       | Default                                                                                                          | Description                                                            |
| -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `address`                  | `Address`                                                  | -                                                                                                                | The Ethereum address to display                                        |
| `disableAddressLink`       | `boolean`                                                  | `false`                                                                                                          | Disable linking to block explorer                                      |
| `format`                   | `"short" \| "long"`                                        | `"short"`                                                                                                        | Display format for the address                                         |
| `size`                     | `"xs" \| "sm" \| "base" \| "lg" \| "xl" \| "2xl" \| "3xl"` | `"base"`                                                                                                         | Size of the component                                                  |
| `chain`                    | `Chain`                                                    | First chain from [WagmiProvider](https://wagmi.sh/react/api/WagmiProvider) config (docs default: Hedera mainnet) | Blockchain network for explorer links                                  |
| `style`                    | `CSSProperties`                                            | -                                                                                                                | Custom CSS styles (memoize to prevent re-renders)                      |
| `blockExplorerAddressLink` | `string`                                                   | -                                                                                                                | Optional override for the block explorer URL used by the address link. |

## Live Examples

### Basic Usage

<DocsProvider>
  <Address address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045" />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<Address address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045" />;
```

### Different Sizes

<DocsProvider>
  <Address
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    size="sm"
  />
  <Address
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    size="xl"
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<Address
  address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  size="sm"
/>;

<Address
  address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  size="xl"
/>;
```

### Long Format

<DocsProvider>
  <Address
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    format="long"
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<Address
  address="0x1234567890123456789012345678901234567890"
  format="long"
/>;
```

### Disable Block Explorer Link

<DocsProvider>
  <Address
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    disableAddressLink={true}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<Address
  address="0x1234567890123456789012345678901234567890"
  disableAddressLink={true}
/>;
```

### Long Format

<DocsProvider>
  <Address
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    format="long"
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<Address
  address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  format="long"
/>;
```

### Custom Chain

import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<DocsProvider>
  <Address
    address="0x1234567890123456789012345678901234567890"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<Address
  address="0x1234567890123456789012345678901234567890"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>;
```

### Invalid Address Handling

When an invalid Ethereum address is provided, the component displays an error message with "Invalid address" text.

<DocsProvider>
  <Address address="0xInvalidAddress123" />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx twoslash
import React from "react";
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

// Invalid address - shows error message
<Address address="0xInvalidAddress123" />;
```

Balance.title: Balance
Balance.body:
import React, { useMemo } from "react";
import { Balance } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { DocsProvider } from "../../components/DocsProvider";

# Balance Component

The `Balance` component displays the native token balance (e.g., ETH, MATIC, HBAR) for an address on a given chain, with support for USD conversion and interactive toggling. On **Hedera** chains (mainnet and testnet), the USD price is sourced from HBAR (CoinGecko); no extra configuration is required.

## Import

```tsx
import { Balance } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
```

## Props

| Prop             | Type            | Default                                                                                                  | Description                                            |
| ---------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `address`        | `Address`       | -                                                                                                        | **Required.** The Ethereum address to show balance for |
| `chain`          | `Chain`         | First chain from [WagmiProvider](https://wagmi.sh/react/api/WagmiProvider) config (docs: Hedera mainnet) | Blockchain network to query                            |
| `defaultUsdMode` | `boolean`       | `false`                                                                                                  | Start in USD display mode                              |
| `style`          | `CSSProperties` | -                                                                                                        | Custom CSS styles (memoize to prevent re-renders)      |

## Live Examples

### Basic Usage

<DocsProvider>
  <Balance address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045" />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<Balance address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045" />
```

### Default USD Mode

<DocsProvider>
  <Balance
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    defaultUsdMode={true}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<Balance
  address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  defaultUsdMode={true}
/>
```

### Custom Chain

import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<DocsProvider>
  <Balance
    address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<Balance
  address="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>;
```

### Hedera (HBAR)

On Hedera chains, pass a Hedera chain (e.g. `hederaTestnet`) and the component uses the HBAR price for USD conversion automatically.

```tsx
import { Balance } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<Balance
  address="0x..."
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>;
```

BaseInput.title: BaseInput

HbarInput.title: HbarInput
HbarInput.body:
import React from "react";
import { HbarInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hedera } from "viem/chains";
import { DocsProvider } from "../../components/DocsProvider";

# HbarInput Component (Hedera-first)

The `HbarInput` component is the primary input for native token/USD amounts. It **defaults to Hedera testnet (HBAR)**. Users can enter a value in HBAR or USD and toggle between them; conversion uses the current HBAR price (CoinGecko).

## Import

```tsx
import { HbarInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
```

## Props

| Prop             | Type                                                                                   | Default         | Description                                              |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `name`           | `string`                                                                               | -               | Name attribute for the input element                     |
| `placeholder`    | `string`                                                                               | -               | Placeholder text for the input                           |
| `defaultValue`   | `string`                                                                               | -               | Initial value for the input                              |
| `defaultUsdMode` | `boolean`                                                                              | `false`         | Start with the input in USD mode instead of native token |
| `chain`          | `Chain`                                                                                | `hederaTestnet` | Chain for price and label (HBAR on Hedera networks).     |
| `disabled`       | `boolean`                                                                              | `false`         | Disables the input and toggle button                     |
| `onValueChange`  | `(value: { valueInNative: string; valueInUsd: string; displayUsdMode: boolean }) => void` | -               | Called when the value or display mode changes (`valueInNative` is HBAR when `chain` is Hedera, etc.) |
| `style`          | `CSSProperties`                                                                        | -               | Custom CSS styles (memoize to prevent re-renders)        |

## Live Examples

### Basic usage (HBAR by default)

<DocsProvider>
  <HbarInput placeholder="Amount in HBAR or USD" />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
import { HbarInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components";

<HbarInput placeholder="Amount in HBAR or USD" />;
```

### Hedera mainnet (`chain`)

<DocsProvider>
  <HbarInput
    chain={hedera}
    placeholder="Amount in HBAR or USD (mainnet)"
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
import { HbarInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hedera } from "viem/chains";

<HbarInput
  chain={hedera}
  placeholder="Amount in HBAR or USD (mainnet)"
/>;
```

### Default USD mode

<DocsProvider>
  <HbarInput
    defaultUsdMode
    placeholder="Enter amount in USD"
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<HbarInput
  defaultUsdMode
  placeholder="Enter amount in USD"
/>
```

## See also

- **[useHbarInput](/hooks/useHbarInput)** — Headless conversion logic used by this component.

HederaAddress.title: HederaAddress
HederaAddress.body:
import React from "react";
import { HederaAddress } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";
import { DocsProvider } from "../../components/DocsProvider";

# HederaAddress Component

The `HederaAddress` component displays a Hedera address with automatic **bidirectional resolution**. Pass a single `value` — either an EVM address (`0x…`) or a native Hedera account ID (`0.0.n`) — and the component resolves the counterpart via the mirror node. It includes a copy button and a link to the block explorer (HashScan for Hedera chains).

## Behavior

- **With EVM address (`0x…`):** Resolves to Hedera account ID via the host's `/api/hedera?evm=...` (or custom resolver). Shows account ID as primary, optional "EVM: 0x..." as secondary. While resolving, shows "Resolving Hedera Account ID…".
- **With native account ID (`0.0.n`):** Resolves to EVM address via `/api/hedera?accountId=...` (or custom resolver). Shows account ID as primary, resolved EVM as secondary. While resolving, shows "Resolving EVM address…".
- **Explorer link:** Uses Hedera-aware URLs (e.g. HashScan `/account/` for Hedera chains) via `getBlockExplorerAddressLink` from `@scaffold-ui/hooks`. Pass **`blockExplorerAddressLink`** to override the URL used for the primary line's link.
- **Invalid value:** If `value` is non-empty but matches neither a valid EVM address nor a valid `0.0.n` pattern, the component shows an error row.

The host app must provide `GET /api/hedera` (or set the appropriate resolvers from `@scaffold-ui/hooks`) for bidirectional resolution.

## Import

```tsx
import { HederaAddress } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
```

## Props

| Prop                       | Type                                                       | Default   | Description                                                                                 |
| -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `value`                    | `string`                                                   | -         | EVM address (`0x…`) or native Hedera account ID (`0.0.n`). Counterpart is auto-resolved.    |
| `chain`                    | `Chain`                                                    | required  | Blockchain network (e.g. `hederaTestnet`) for resolution and explorer link.                 |
| `format`                   | `"short" \| "long"`                                        | `"short"` | Display format for the address (truncated vs full).                                         |
| `size`                     | `"xs" \| "sm" \| "base" \| "lg" \| "xl" \| "2xl" \| "3xl"` | `"base"`  | Typography and avatar size.                                                                 |
| `disableAddressLink`       | `boolean`                                                  | `false`   | Disable the link to the block explorer.                                                     |
| `avatarComponent`          | `ComponentType<{ address, size, ensImage }>`               | -         | Optional component to render an avatar (e.g. blockie) instead of the default placeholder.   |
| `showEvmAddress`           | `boolean`                                                  | `true`    | When account ID is primary, show "EVM: 0x..." as secondary.                                 |
| `style`                    | `CSSProperties`                                            | -         | Custom styles on the root wrapper (memoize to prevent re-renders).                          |
| `blockExplorerAddressLink` | `string`                                                   | -         | Overrides the computed block explorer URL for the primary display link.                     |

## Live Examples

### Basic usage (EVM address)

<DocsProvider>
  <HederaAddress
    value="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
import { HederaAddress } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<HederaAddress
  value="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>;
```

### Native account ID (resolves EVM from mirror)

<DocsProvider>
  <HederaAddress
    value="0.0.8392910"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<HederaAddress
  value="0.0.8392910"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>
```

### Long format

<DocsProvider>
  <HederaAddress
    value="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
    format="long"
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<HederaAddress
  value="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
  format="long"
/>
```

### Disable block explorer link

<DocsProvider>
  <HederaAddress
    value="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
    disableAddressLink={true}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<HederaAddress
  value="0xd8da6bf26964af9d7eed9e03e53415d37aa96045"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
  disableAddressLink={true}
/>
```

### Hide EVM address (account ID only)

<DocsProvider>
  <HederaAddress
    value="0.0.8392910"
    chain={hederaTestnet}
    showEvmAddress={false}
  />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<HederaAddress
  value="0.0.8392910"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
  showEvmAddress={false}
/>
```

### Loading state (no value)

When `value` is not provided, the component shows a loading skeleton.

<DocsProvider>
  <HederaAddress chain={hederaTestnet} />
</DocsProvider>

```tsx
<HederaAddress chain={hederaTestnet} />
```

## Related

- **[Address](/components/Address)** – Display Ethereum addresses (ENS, blockie, copy).
- **[HederaAddressInput](/components/HederaAddressInput)** – Input for Hedera (EVM or native account ID) with resolution.
- **Hooks:** Resolution uses `useHederaAccountId` (EVM → account ID) and `useHederaEvmAddress` (account ID → EVM) from `@scaffold-ui/hooks`. Override with `setHederaAccountIdResolver` / `setHederaEvmAddressResolver` or their API base setters.

HederaAddressInput.title: HederaAddressInput
HederaAddressInput.body:
import React from "react"; import { DocsProvider } from "../../components/DocsProvider"; import { HederaAddressInputExample } from "../../components/HederaAddressInputExample"; # HederaAddressInput Component The `HederaAddressInput` component is an input for Hedera that accepts **two formats**: EVM addresses (`0x` + 40 hex characters) and **native Hedera account IDs** (e.g. `0.0.12345`). The **text in the field stays what the user typed**; the inline suffix shows the other representation. Use optional `onResolvedEvmChange` when you need a checksummed EVM address for contract calls. Hedera **long-zero** EVM aliases (e.g. `0x000…001c` for `0.0.28`) are accepted with **non-strict** address checks: explorers may show mixed-case hex that does not satisfy EIP-55, which viem would otherwise reject in strict mode. ## Behavior | Input format | Resolution | Display | `onChange` / suffix | | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **EVM** (`0x...`) | Validated; then resolved to Hedera account ID for suffix | Input stays `0x…`; suffix `→ Resolving…` then `→ 0.0.12345` | `onChange` = raw string; copy suffix copies account ID | | **Native** (`0.0.12345`) | Resolved to EVM via mirror/API | Input stays `0.0.n`; suffix `→ 0xAbCd…1234` (short); copy copies **full** EVM | `onChange` = raw string; `onResolvedEvmChange` gets checksummed EVM when valid | - **Errors** (blocking): Invalid EVM length/format, bare 40-char hex without `0x`, malformed `0.0.n`, plain digits without dots (e.g. `123`), random text, account not found on Hedera. - **Warnings** (non-blocking): Native account with no EVM alias; EVM address with no mirror mapping on this network; mirror verification failure. - **Disabled:** The field is temporarily disabled only while **resolving native account ID → EVM** (so the raw `0.0.n` text does not change mid-flight). While **EVM → account ID** is resolving, the input stays enabled; the **pending** border and suffix (`Resolving…`) show progress instead. - **UI**: `BaseInput` — default border when idle or resolved (suffix shows conversion and a **copy** control for the resolved value), **pending** uses a neutral `base-content` tint on the border while resolving, **error** uses the input error token. The outer wrapper sets **`aria-busy`** while a lookup is in progress and there is no blocking error (assistive tech can treat the field as busy during resolution). The host app must provide resolution endpoints (or custom resolvers from `@scaffold-ui/hooks`): - **EVM → account ID:** same-origin `GET /api/hedera?evm=...&network=testnet|mainnet` returning `{ accountId }`. - **Account ID → EVM:** same-origin `GET /api/hedera?accountId=...&network=...` returning `{ evmAddress }`. ## Import ```tsx import { HederaAddressInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components"; ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `value` | `string` | - | Current value: EVM address or native account ID string. | | `onChange` | `(value: string) => void` | - | Called with the **raw** input text on every change (native ID or `0x` string). | | `onResolvedEvmChange` | `(address: Address \| undefined) => void` | - | Optional: notified when the resolved checksummed EVM address changes (undefined if invalid / cleared). | | `name` | `string` | - | Name attribute of the input element. | | `placeholder` | `string` | `"0.0.12345 or 0x…"` | Placeholder text. | | `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | When `true`, disables the input. Otherwise, the input is disabled only during **native → EVM** resolution (see Behavior). | | `style` | `CSSProperties` | - | Custom CSS styles (memoize to prevent re-renders). | | `chainId` | `number` | `296` (testnet) | Chain ID for resolution (296 = Hedera testnet, 295 = mainnet). | ## Live Examples ### Basic usage ```tsx import React, { useState } from "react"; import type { Address } from "viem"; import { HederaAddressInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components"; function ParentComponent() { const [value, setValue] = useState(""); const [resolvedAddress, setResolvedAddress] = useState
(undefined); return (
{resolvedAddress &&

Resolved EVM: {resolvedAddress}

}
); } ``` ### With chainId (mainnet) ```tsx import { HederaAddressInput } from "@scaffold-ui/components"; ; ``` ### Disabled state ```tsx {}} disabled /> ``` ## Related - **[Address](/components/Address)** – Display an address with blockie and copy. - **[useHederaAddressInput](/hooks/useHederaAddressInput)** — Headless validation and resolution used by this component. - **Hooks:** Resolution uses the same endpoints as the account ID ↔ EVM resolvers in `@scaffold-ui/hooks`. Override with `setHederaAccountIdResolver` (EVM → account ID) or `setHederaEvmAddressResolver` (account ID → EVM).

Styling.title: Styling

Theming.title: Theming

index.title: index
index.body:
# Components

Pre-built React components for common Hedera UI patterns.

## Installation

### Peer Dependencies

Install the required peer dependencies using your preferred package manager:

:::code-group

```bash [npm]
npm install react react-dom @types/react viem wagmi @tanstack/react-query
```

```bash [pnpm]
pnpm add react react-dom @types/react viem wagmi @tanstack/react-query
```

```bash [yarn]
yarn add react react-dom @types/react viem wagmi @tanstack/react-query
```

:::

### Package Installation

Install Scaffold UI components and hooks using your preferred package manager:

:::code-group

```bash [npm]
npm install @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks
```

```bash [pnpm]
pnpm add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks
```

```bash [yarn]
yarn add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks
```

:::

### Import Styles

Import the styles in your root component file (`app.tsx` for React or `layout.tsx` for Next.js):

```typescript
import "@scaffold-ui/components/styles.css";
```

### Create wagmi config and setup Tanstack query

https://wagmi.sh/react/getting-started#create-config

useAddress.title: useAddress

useBalance.title: useBalance

useCreateToken.title: useCreateToken
useCreateToken.body:
# useCreateToken Hook

`useCreateToken` submits a **Hedera Token Service (HTS) fungible token create** via [`useNativeTransaction`](/hooks/useNativeTransaction). It builds a `TokenCreateTransaction` (0 decimals, infinite supply, treasury = your chosen account), validates name/symbol/supply, and returns metadata plus `transactionId`.

The hook does **not** read the connected account by itself. You pass **`getTreasuryAccountId`** so the treasury is always explicit (typically the connected account’s `0.0.x` id).

## Peer dependency

Requires **`@hiero-ledger/sdk`** (same major as your app).

```bash
pnpm add @hiero-ledger/sdk
```

## Import

```tsx
import {
  useCreateToken,
  type CreateTokenParams,
  type CreateTokenResult,
  type UseCreateTokenOptions,
} from "@scaffold-ui/hooks";
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { useCreateToken } from "@scaffold-ui/hooks";
import { useHederaSigner } from "./useHederaSigner";

function CreateTokenPanel() {
  const { requireProvider } = useHederaSigner();
  const createToken = useCreateToken({
    getTreasuryAccountId: () => requireProvider().accountId,
  });

  return (
    <button
      type="button"
      disabled={createToken.isPending}
      onClick={() =>
        createToken.mutate({
          name: "My Token",
          symbol: "MTK",
          initialSupply: "1000",
        })
      }
    >
      {createToken.isPending ? "Creating…" : "Create token"}
    </button>
  );
}
```

## Options

| Option                    | Type            | Description                                                                 |
| ------------------------- | --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `getTreasuryAccountId`    | `() => string`  | Return the Hedera account id (`0.0.n`) to use as treasury; throw if unavailable. |

## Mutation variables (`CreateTokenParams`)

| Property          | Type       | Default | Description                                      |
| ----------------- | ---------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `name`            | `string`   | —       | Token name (trimmed, max 100 characters).        |
| `symbol`          | `string`   | —       | Trimmed, uppercased; must match `^[A-Z0-9]{1,10}$`. |
| `initialSupply?`  | `string`   | `"0"`   | Non-negative integer as string or number-like.   |

## Token shape (library defaults)

The built transaction uses:

- **Type:** fungible common  
- **Decimals:** `0`  
- **Supply type:** infinite  
- **Treasury:** `AccountId.fromString(getTreasuryAccountId())`

Adjust the SDK in your own code if you need different decimals or supply type.

## Return value

Returns a **TanStack Query `useMutation`** result.

On success, **`data`** (`CreateTokenResult`) is:

| Property               | Type     | Description                          |
| ---------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `transactionId`        | `string` | From the wallet.                     |
| `name`                 | `string` | Normalized name.                     |
| `symbol`               | `string` | Normalized symbol.                   |
| `initialSupply`        | `number` | Parsed supply.                       |
| `treasuryAccountId`    | `string` | Value from `getTreasuryAccountId()`. |

## Validation errors

The mutation throws if:

- Name or symbol missing, name too long, or symbol not 1–10 uppercase alphanumeric.
- `initialSupply` is not a finite non-negative integer.

## Other errors

- **`CapabilityError`** — No native signer ([`useNativeTransaction`](/hooks/useNativeTransaction)).
- **`Error("No transactionId returned from wallet")`** — Empty wallet response.
- Any error thrown from **`getTreasuryAccountId`** (e.g. wallet not connected).

useCreateTopic.title: useCreateTopic

useHbarInput.title: useHbarInput
useHbarInput.body:
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useHbarInput } from "@scaffold-ui/hooks";
import { DocsProvider } from "../../components/DocsProvider";

# useHbarInput Hook (Hedera-first)

The `useHbarInput` hook converts between native token and USD for a given input and display mode. It **defaults to Hedera testnet (HBAR)**. It returns both values, the current native currency price, and loading/error flags. Pass a different `chain` (e.g. `hedera` from `viem/chains`) to use Hedera mainnet pricing.

## Import

```tsx
import { useHbarInput } from "@scaffold-ui/hooks";
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { hedera } from "viem/chains";

const {
  valueInNative,
  valueInUsd,
  nativeCurrencyPrice,
  isNativeCurrencyPriceLoading,
  isNativeCurrencyPriceError,
  nativeCurrencySymbol,
} = useHbarInput({ value, usdMode });
// Optional: Hedera mainnet (default is hederaTestnet)
useHbarInput({ value, usdMode, chain: hedera });
```

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type      | Default         | Description                                                                         |
| --------- | --------- | --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `value`   | `string`  | -               | The current input value. Interpreted as native token or USD depending on `usdMode`. |
| `usdMode` | `boolean` | -               | If `true`, `value` is USD; if `false`, `value` is native token (e.g. HBAR).         |
| `chain`   | `Chain`   | `hederaTestnet` | Chain for price and symbol (HBAR on Hedera chains).                                 |

## Return Values

| Property                       | Type      | Description                                                                             |
| ------------------------------ | --------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `valueInNative`                | `string`  | The input value expressed in native token (HBAR on Hedera chains). Empty string when conversion is unavailable. |
| `valueInUsd`                   | `string`  | The input value expressed in USD. Empty string when conversion is unavailable.          |
| `nativeCurrencyPrice`          | `number`  | The fetched native currency price in USD. `0` when not yet available.                   |
| `nativeCurrencySymbol`         | `string`  | Symbol for the chain (e.g. `"HBAR"`).                                                   |
| `error`                        | `string \| null` | Conversion error message (e.g. invalid input); `null` when no error.                 |
| `isError`                      | `boolean` | `true` when a conversion error occurred.                                                |
| `isNativeCurrencyPriceLoading` | `boolean` | Loading state for fetching the price.                                                   |
| `isNativeCurrencyPriceError`   | `boolean` | Error state for fetching the price.                                                     |

useHederaEvmAddress.title: useHederaEvmAddress
useHederaEvmAddress.body:
# useHederaEvmAddress Hook The `useHederaEvmAddress` hook resolves an EVM address (`0x…`) for a Hedera native account ID (`0.0.n`) using the mirror node (same-origin `/api/hedera` or injected resolver). ## Import ```tsx import { useHederaEvmAddress, type HederaEvmAddressStatus } from "@scaffold-ui/hooks"; ``` ## Usage ```tsx const { evmAddress, status, isLoading } = useHederaEvmAddress("0.0.8392910", 296); ``` ## Parameters | Parameter | Type | Default | Description | | ----------- | --------------------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `accountId` | `string \| undefined` | - | Hedera native account ID (e.g. `"0.0.12345"`). Must match `\d+\.\d+\.\d+`. | | `chainId` | `number` _(optional)_ | `296` | Chain ID for network mapping (`296` testnet, `295` mainnet). | ## Return Values | Property | Type | Description | | ------------ | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `evmAddress` | `Address \| undefined` | Checksummed EVM address when resolution succeeds; `undefined` otherwise. | | `status` | `HederaEvmAddressStatus` | One of `"idle"`, `"pending"`, `"success"`, `"not-found"`, `"error"`. | | `isLoading` | `boolean` | `true` while the first fetch is in flight (no cached data yet). | ### Status values | Status | Meaning | | ------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `"idle"` | No account ID to resolve (input cleared or invalid format). | | `"pending"` | First fetch in flight for this query (no cached data yet). | | `"success"` | Mirror returned an EVM address (`evmAddress` is set). | | `"not-found"` | Mirror responded: no EVM alias for this account (e.g. ED25519-only key). | | `"error"` | Request failed (network, 5xx, thrown resolver, etc.). | ## Example ```tsx import React from "react"; import { useHederaEvmAddress } from "@scaffold-ui/hooks"; function EvmLookup({ accountId }: { accountId: string }) { const { evmAddress, status, isLoading } = useHederaEvmAddress(accountId, 296); if (isLoading) return

Resolving…

; return (

Status: {status}

{evmAddress ?

EVM: {evmAddress}

:

No EVM alias found.

}
); } ``` ## Related - **[useHederaAccountId](/hooks/useHederaAccountId)** — Reverse direction: EVM address → Hedera account ID. - **[HederaAddress](/components/HederaAddress)** — Display component that uses both hooks for bidirectional resolution. - **Resolver setup:** Configure with `setHederaEvmAddressResolver` or `setHederaEvmAddressApiBase` from `@scaffold-ui/hooks`. Default: same-origin `GET /api/hedera?accountId=…&network=…` returning `{ evmAddress }`.

useMirrorNodeAccount.title: useMirrorNodeAccount
useMirrorNodeAccount.body:
# useMirrorNodeAccount Hook The `useMirrorNodeAccount` hook is the **single source of truth** for Hedera account data from the Mirror Node. It accepts either an EVM address (`0x...`) or a native account ID (`0.0.XXXXX`) and returns identity, key type, and balance in one cached request. Multiple components using the same input share one fetch via React Query. ## Import ```tsx import { useMirrorNodeAccount, type HederaAccount, type HederaKeyType, type UseMirrorNodeAccountOptions, } from "@scaffold-ui/hooks"; ``` ## Usage ```tsx const { data, isLoading, isError, refetch } = useMirrorNodeAccount("0.0.2", { network: "testnet", }); // Or with chainId (296 = testnet, 295 = mainnet) const result = useMirrorNodeAccount(accountIdOrEvm, { chainId: 296 }); // Skip the request when input is undefined const result = useMirrorNodeAccount(undefined); // → data: null, isLoading: false ``` ## Parameters | Parameter | Type | Default | Description | | --------- | ----------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `input` | `string \| undefined` | - | EVM address (`0x...`) or Hedera account ID (`0.0.12345`). When `undefined`, no request is made. | | `options` | `UseMirrorNodeAccountOptions` | `{}` | Optional config (see below). | ### Options | Option | Type | Description | | --------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `network` | `"testnet" \| "mainnet"` | Override network (otherwise derived from `chainId`). | | `chainId` | `number` | Chain ID to derive network (296 = testnet, 295 = mainnet). Defaults to testnet. | | `fetcher` | `MirrorNodeAccountFetcher` | Custom fetcher; overrides the default API call. | ## Return Values | Property | Type | Description | | ----------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | `data` | `HederaAccount \| null` | Resolved account data, or `null` when not found / not yet loaded. | | `isLoading` | `boolean` | `true` while the request is in flight. | | `isError` | `boolean` | `true` when the request failed. | | `refetch` | `() => void` | Trigger a new fetch (e.g. after an error). | ## HederaAccount Type | Field | Type | Description | | ------------ | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | `accountId` | `string` | Hedera account ID (e.g. `"0.0.12345"`). | | `evmAddress` | `string \| null` | EVM alias if present; `null` for ED25519-only accounts. | | `keyType` | `"ED25519" \| "ECDSA_SECP256K1"` | Account's public key type. | | `balance` | `bigint` | Balance in **tinybars** (8 decimals). | | `canSignEVM` | `boolean` | `true` when the account has an EVM alias (`evmAddress !== null`). | ## Backend: Default API or Custom Fetcher By default the hook calls **same-origin** `${apiBase}/api/hedera?input=...&network=...`. The host app must implement that route (e.g. by proxying to the [Hedera Mirror Node REST API](https://docs.hedera.com/api-reference/accounts/get-account-by-alias-id-or-evm-address)) and return JSON: - `accountId` (string) - `evmAddress` (string | null) - `keyType` (`"ED25519"` | `"ECDSA_SECP256K1"`) - `balance` (string or number, tinybars) You can: - **Set API base:** `setMirrorNodeAccountApiBase("https://your-app.com")` so the default fetch uses that origin. - **Inject a custom fetcher:** `setMirrorNodeAccountFetcher(async (input, network) => { ... return HederaAccount | null; })` so the hook never calls the default URL (e.g. use your own backend or mirror client). ## Caching The hook uses **React Query** with: - **Query key:** `["mirrorNode", "account", normalizedInput, network]` - **staleTime:** 30 seconds - **gcTime:** 5 minutes Components that use the same `input` and `network` share one request. ## Example ```tsx function AccountSummary({ accountIdOrEvm }: { accountIdOrEvm: string }) { const { data, isLoading, isError, refetch } = useMirrorNodeAccount(accountIdOrEvm, { network: "testnet", }); if (isLoading) return Loading…; if (isError || !data) return ; const hbar = Number(data.balance) / 1e8; return (

Account: {data.accountId}

EVM: {data.evmAddress ?? "—"}

Key: {data.keyType}

Balance: {hbar.toFixed(4)} HBAR

); } ``` A full working demo (with `GET /api/hedera`) is in the [example app](https://github.com/buidler-labs/scaffold-ui/tree/main/example).

gettingStarted:
# Scaffold UI

**Scaffold-UI** exposes a clean set of components and hooks that give you everything you need to build Hedera dApps.

## Components

Pre-built React components for common Hedera UI patterns:

- **[Address](/components/Address)** - Display EVM-format addresses with blockie, copy, and explorer link (Hedera-aware when using Hedera chains)
- **[HederaAddress](/components/HederaAddress)** - Display Hedera addresses with account ID resolution and HashScan link
- **[HederaAddressInput](/components/HederaAddressInput)** - Input for Hedera (EVM or native 0.0.12345) with bidirectional resolution
- **[Balance](/components/Balance)** - Display native token balances with optional USD toggle (HBAR on Hedera chains)
- **[HbarInput](/components/HbarInput)** - Input for HBAR (or native token) amounts with USD conversion (Hedera-first, defaults to HBAR)

**[View Installation Guide →](/components#installation)**

## Hooks

Our components are built on top of these React hooks, which you can use directly to create your own custom Hedera components, or even combine them to build more complex ones.

- **[useMirrorNodeAccount](/hooks/useMirrorNodeAccount)** - Single source of truth for Hedera account data (identity, key type, balance) from the Mirror Node
- **[useNativeTransaction](/hooks/useNativeTransaction)** - Send native Hedera transactions (HTS, HCS, CryptoTransfer) via the Hedera namespace; throws CapabilityError when no signer is set
- **[useHbarInput](/hooks/useHbarInput)** - Convert between native token (default HBAR) and USD (Hedera-first)
- **[useHederaAddressInput](/hooks/useHederaAddressInput)** - Validate and resolve Hedera address input (native or EVM); headless logic for `HederaAddressInput`
- **[useAddress](/hooks/useAddress)** - Address display helpers and block explorer links for a given chain
- **[useBalance](/hooks/useBalance)** - Fetch and watch address balances with USD conversion toggle

**[View Installation Guide →](/hooks#installation)**

## Debug Contracts

**[Debug Contracts](/debug-contracts/Contract)** - A contract UI component for debugging deployed contracts.

packageReadmes.components:
# @scaffold-ui/components

React components for scaffold-ui applications.

## Installation

**Note**: This package requires `@scaffold-ui/hooks` as a peer dependency.

```bash
# Install both packages
npm install @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks

# Or with yarn
yarn add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks

# Or with pnpm
pnpm add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks
```

### Peer Dependencies

You'll also need these peer dependencies if you don't already have them:

```bash
npm install react @types/react viem wagmi @tanstack/react-query
```

## Components

### Address

Displays an EVM-format address with blockie, copy, and block explorer link. Pass a Hedera chain from `viem/chains` for HashScan URLs.

#### Props

- `address?` - The address to display (optional)
- `disableAddressLink?` - Disable the block explorer link (optional)
- `format?` - `"short"` | `"long"` (optional)
- `size?` - `"xs"` | `"sm"` | `"base"` | `"lg"` | `"xl"` | `"2xl"` | `"3xl"` (default `"base"`)
- `chain?` - Viem `Chain` for explorer links (optional; can default from Wagmi config)
- `style?`, `blockExplorerAddressLink?` - Optional styling / URL override

#### Example

```tsx
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<Address
  address="0x1234567890123456789012345678901234567890"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>;
```

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Build the package
pnpm build

# Watch for changes during development
pnpm dev

# Lint the code
pnpm lint

# Format the code
pnpm format
```

installationSnippet:
## Packages

- `@scaffold-ui/components` — UI components
- `@scaffold-ui/hooks` — React hooks (required peer of components)
- `@scaffold-ui/debug-contracts` — Contract debug UI

### Install

```bash
pnpm add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks
# optional:
pnpm add @scaffold-ui/debug-contracts
```

### Peer dependencies

React 19, `viem`, `wagmi`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `@types/react`.
For Hedera native transaction hooks: `@hiero-ledger/sdk`.
Import styles once at app root:

```ts
import "@scaffold-ui/components/styles.css";
import "@scaffold-ui/debug-contracts/styles.css"; // if using debug UI
```

### Package READMEs (summary)

# @scaffold-ui/components

React components for scaffold-ui applications.

## Installation

**Note**: This package requires `@scaffold-ui/hooks` as a peer dependency.

```bash
# Install both packages
npm install @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks

# Or with yarn
yarn add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks

# Or with pnpm
pnpm add @scaffold-ui/components @scaffold-ui/hooks
```

### Peer Dependencies

You'll also need these peer dependencies if you don't already have them:

```bash
npm install react @types/react viem wagmi @tanstack/react-query
```

## Components

### Address

Displays an EVM-format address with blockie, copy, and block explorer link. Pass a Hedera chain from `viem/chains` for HashScan URLs.

#### Props

- `address?` - The address to display (optional)
- `disableAddressLink?` - Disable the block explorer link (optional)
- `format?` - `"short"` | `"long"` (optional)
- `size?` - `"xs"` | `"sm"` | `"base"` | `"lg"` | `"xl"` | `"2xl"` | `"3xl"` (default `"base"`)
- `chain?` - Viem `Chain` for explorer links (optional; can default from Wagmi config)
- `style?`, `blockExplorerAddressLink?` - Optional styling / URL override

#### Example

```tsx
import { Address } from "@scaffold-ui/components";
import { hederaTestnet } from "viem/chains";

<Address
  address="0x1234567890123456789012345678901234567890"
  chain={hederaTestnet}
/>;
```

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Build the package
pnpm build

# Watch for changes during development

# Lint the code

# Format the code

```

useBalance.jsdoc:
/**
 * useBalance Hook
 *
 * Fetches and watches the balance of a given address on a specified chain, with USD conversion.
 * - Retrieves the native token balance for the provided address and chain.
 * - Fetches the USD price and allows toggling between native and USD display.
 *
 * @param {UseBalanceOptions} options - Options for fetching the balance.
 * @param {Address} [options.address] - The address to fetch the balance for.
 * @param {Chain} [options.chain=mainnet] - The blockchain network to use. Defaults to Ethereum mainnet.
 * @param {boolean} [options.defaultUsdMode=false] - (Optional) If true, displays the balance in USD by default. If the price is not fetched, the balance is displayed in native currency.
 *
 * @returns {Object} An object containing:
 *   - displayUsdMode {boolean}: Whether the balance is currently displayed in USD.
 *   - toggleDisplayUsdMode {() => void}: Function to toggle between native and USD display.
 *   - formattedBalance {number}: The balance in native currency, formatted as a number.
 *   - balanceInUsd {number}: The balance converted to USD.
 *   - balance {object|undefined}: The raw balance object (from wagmi).
 *   - isBalanceError {boolean}: Error state for balance fetching.
 *   - isBalanceLoading {boolean}: Loading state for balance fetching.
 *   - isNativeCurrencyPriceLoading {boolean}: Loading state for price fetching.
 *   - isNativeCurrencyPriceError {boolean}: Error state for price fetching.
 *   - isLoading {boolean}: Combined loading state.
 *   - isError {boolean}: Combined error state.
 *
 * @example
 * const { formattedBalance, balanceInUsd, displayUsdMode, toggleDisplayUsdMode } = useBalance({
 *   address: "0x123...",
 *   chain: mainnet,
 *   defaultUsdMode: true,
 * });
 */

useWatchBalance.jsdoc:
/**
 * useWatchBalance Hook
 *
 * Watches and updates the balance of an address on every new block for a given chain.
 *
 * @param {UseWatchBalanceOptions} useBalanceParameters - Parameters for fetching the balance, including address and optional chain.
 *
 * @returns {Object} The return value of wagmi's useBalance hook, containing:
 *   - data {object|undefined}: The balance data object.
 *   - isError {boolean}: Error state for balance fetching.
 *   - isLoading {boolean}: Loading state for balance fetching.
 *   - ...other wagmi useBalance return values.
 *
 * @example
 * const { data, isLoading } = useWatchBalance({ address: "0x123...", chain });
 */

useFetchNativeCurrencyPrice.jsdoc:
/**
 * useFetchNativeCurrencyPrice Hook
 *
 * Fetches the current USD price of the native currency for a given chain.
 * - For Hedera chains (mainnet and testnet), uses CoinGecko HBAR price.
 * - For other chains, uses Uniswap V2 on mainnet; set `nativeCurrencyTokenAddress` in `NETWORKS_EXTRA_DATA[chain.id]` for non-ETH natives.
 *
 * @param {Chain} [chain=mainnet] - (Optional) The blockchain network to fetch the native currency price for.
 *
 * @returns {Object} An object containing:
 *   - price {number}: The current price of the native currency in USD.
 *   - isLoading {boolean}: Loading state for the price fetching process.
 *   - error {unknown}: Error object if fetching fails.
 *   - isError {boolean}: Error state for the price fetching process.
 *
 * @example
 * const { price, isLoading, isError } = useFetchNativeCurrencyPrice(mainnet);
 * const { price: hbarPrice } = useFetchNativeCurrencyPrice(hederaTestnet);
 */

useHederaAccountId.jsdoc:
/**
 * Outcome of resolving an EVM address to a Hedera account ID via the host mirror/API.
 *
 * - `idle` — no address to resolve (input cleared).
 * - `pending` — first fetch in flight for this query (no cached data yet).
 * - `success` — mirror returned an account ID (`accountId` is set).
 * - `not-found` — mirror responded: no Hedera account for this EVM on this network.
 * - `error` — request failed (network, 5xx, thrown resolver, etc.).
 */
export type HederaAccountIdStatus = "idle" | "pending" | "success" | "not-found" | "error";

/**
 * Resolves a Hedera account ID (`0.0.n`) for a checksummed EVM address using `getHederaAccountId`
 * (same-origin `/api/hedera` or injected resolver). Uses TanStack Query for caching, deduplication,
 * and abort when the address/chain changes (same pattern as `useFetchNativeCurrencyPrice`).
 */

useHederaEvmAddress.jsdoc:
/**
 * Outcome of resolving a Hedera account ID to an EVM address via the host mirror/API.
 *
 * - `idle` — no account ID to resolve (input cleared or invalid format).
 * - `pending` — first fetch in flight for this query (no cached data yet).
 * - `success` — mirror returned an EVM address (`evmAddress` is set).
 * - `not-found` — mirror responded: no EVM alias for this account (e.g. ED25519-only).
 * - `error` — request failed (network, 5xx, thrown resolver, etc.).
 */
export type HederaEvmAddressStatus = "idle" | "pending" | "success" | "not-found" | "error";

/**
 * Resolves an EVM address (`0x…`) for a Hedera native account ID (`0.0.n`) using
 * `getEvmAddressFromHederaAccountId` (same-origin `/api/hedera` or injected resolver).
 * Uses TanStack Query for caching, deduplication, and abort on param changes.
 */
